revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24744680692|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Hikite Chikara no Mikoto Shrine]][[ja:引手力命神社]] {{Expand Japanese|引手力命神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hikite Chikara no Mikoto Shrine | native_name = 引手力命神社 | image = Hikitechikaranomikoto Jinja (Haiden).jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hikite Chikara no Mikoto Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.028139|138.786472}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | established = {'time': '+0684-00-00T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 9, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985786'} | website = {{Official website|http://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/kankou/asobu/osezaki/index.htm}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hikite Chikara no Mikoto Shrine'''|引手力命神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|ja|伊豆国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11380552|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}} ** Series ordinal: 63 ** P155: {{ill|Q134926921|ltq=Q134926921|qid=Q134926921|1=Nagoya Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q134926458|ltq=Q134926458|qid=Q134926458|1=Hatsuhime Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Numazu|lt=Numazu|ar|نامازو (شيزوكا)|arz|نامازو|azb|نومازو، شیزوئوکا|bg|Нумадзу|ceb|Numazu (lungsod sa Hapon)|ce|Нумадзу|de|Numazu|en|Numazu|envoyage|Numazu|eo|Numazu|es|Numazu (Shizuoka)|et|Numazu|fa|نومازو، شیزوئوکا|fi|Numazu|fr|Numazu|ga|Numazu|gl|Numazu|hr|Numazu|id|Numazu, Shizuoka|io|Numazu, Shizuoka|it|Numazu|ja|沼津市|javoyage|沼津市|ko|누마즈시|mg|Numazu|mn|Нүмазү|mzn|نومازو، شیزوئوکا|nl|Numazu|no|Numazu|pl|Numazu|pt|Numazu|ro|Numazu|ru|Нумадзу|sh|Numazu|simple|Numazu|sr|Нумазу|sv|Numazu|szl|Numazu|tg|Нумазу|th|นูมาซุ|tl|Numazu|tr|Numazu|tt|Нумадзу|uk|Нумадзу (Сідзуока)|ur|نومازو، شیزوکا|vi|Numazu, Shizuoka|war|Numazu, Shizuoka|zh_classical|沼津市|zh_min_nan|Numazu-chhī|zh_yue|沼津|zh|沼津市|qid=Q241037|1=Numazu}} * {{ill|Q860928|ltq=Q860928|qid=Q860928|1=Izu Province}} * {{ill|Tagata District, Shizuoka|lt=Tagata district|en|Tagata District, Shizuoka|fr|District de Tagata|id|Distrik Tagata, Shizuoka|it|Distretto di Tagata|ja|田方郡|ko|다가타군|pl|Powiat Tagata|uk|Повіт Таґата|zh|田方郡|qid=Q1154841|1=Tagata District, Shizuoka}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.028139|138.786472}} == inception (P571) == * {'time': '+0684-00-00T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 9, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985786'} == official website (P856) == * http://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/kankou/asobu/osezaki/index.htm ** Language of work: {{ill|Q5287|ltq=Q5287|qid=Q5287|1=Japanese language}} == image (P18) == * Hikitechikaranomikoto Jinja (Haiden).jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181631 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Gō-sha|lt=Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == legal form (P1454) == * {{ill|Q7311343|ltq=Q7311343|qid=Q7311343|1=religious corporation}} == country (P17) == * ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * ひきたぢからのみことじんじゃ * ひきてちからのみことじんじゃ * ヒキタチカラノ-Engishiki Jinmyōchō == located in or next to body of water (P206) == * {{ill|Suruga Bay|lt=Suruga Bay|ar|خليج سوروغا|be|Суруга (заліў)|bg|Суруга|ceb|Suruga Wan|de|Suruga-Bucht|en|Suruga Bay|eo|Golfo Suruga|es|Bahía de Suruga|eu|Suruga Badia|fa|خلیج سوروگا|fr|Baie de Suruga|he|מפרץ סורוגה|it|Baia di Suruga|ja|駿河湾|ko|스루가만|no|Surugabukta|pl|Suruga (zatoka)|ru|Суруга (залив)|simple|Suruga Bay|th|อ่าวซูรูงะ|tr|Suruga Körfezi|zh|駿河灣|qid=Q1201023|1=Suruga Bay}} == postal code (P281) == * 410-0244 == TripAdvisor ID (P3134) == * 7942655 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8080105000285 == Commons category (P373) == * Hikite Chikara no Mikoto Shrine == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039675|ltq=Q135039675|qid=Q135039675|1=Hikitachikarano- Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県沼津市西浦江梨329', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '静岡県伊東市十足', 'language': 'ja'} == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Q11437688|ltq=Q11437688|qid=Q11437688|1=Cape Ose}} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Hikitechikaranomikoto Jinja (Torii).jpg {{commons category|Category:Hikite Chikara no Mikoto Shrine}} [[JA:引手力命神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11487151}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Hikite Chikara no Mikoto Shrine |画像=[[画像:Hikitejikaranomikotojinja.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (honden is behind this)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|1|41.3|N|138|47|11.3|E}}}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|qid=Q241037|1=Numazu}}Nishiura Enashi 329 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 1 | 緯度秒 = 41.3 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 47 | 経度秒 = 11.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | ISO = JP-22 | 地図 = Japan Shizuoka | ラベル位置 = bottom |祭神=Hikite Chikara no Mikoto |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|qid=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 13 ({{ill|lt=684|Q31128|qid=Q31128|1=684}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名=Ōse Shrine |例祭={{ill|lt=April 4|Q2508|qid=Q2508|1=April 4}} |}} [[ファイル:Hikitechikaranomikoto Jinja (Torii).jpg|thumb|270px|Torii]] [[ファイル:Hikitechikaranomikoto Jinja.jpg|thumb|270px|Torii and saikan / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine office|1=UNKNOWN}}]] '''Hikite Chikara no Mikoto Shrine''' (ひきてちからのみことじんじゃ / ひきたぢからのみことじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} at {{ill|lt=Ōsezaki|Q11437688|qid=Q11437688|1=Cape Ose}} in Nishiura Enashi, {{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|qid=Q241037|1=Numazu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its former rank was shikinaisha candidate shrine (small government-offering shrine candidate) and gōsha. === Overview === It has long drawn devotion from fishing communities along {{ill|lt=Suruga Bay|Q1201023|qid=Q1201023|1=Suruga Bay}}{{Sfn|Miyata|p=29}}. The "Hikite Chikara no Mikoto Shrine" listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} is often identified with this shrine, but another shrine exists in former Tagata District of {{ill|lt=Izu Province|Q860928|qid=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}: {{ill|lt=Hikite Chikarao Shrine|Q134956261|qid=Q134956261|1=Hikite Chikarao Shrine}} (ひきたじからおじんじゃ) in Tōbosoku, {{ill|lt=Itō|Q721163|qid=Q721163|1=Itō, Shizuoka}} (north foot of Mt. {{ill|lt=Ōmuro|Q11434371|qid=Q11434371|1=Mount Ōmuro (Shizuoka)}}). Because no decisive historical source establishes which one corresponds to the shikinaisha "Hikite Chikara no Mikoto Shrine," the matter is unresolved. This article covers the shrine at Ōsezaki. In {{ill|lt=2006|Q2021|qid=Q2021|1=2006}}, it was selected (under the facility name "Ōse Shrine") as one of the {{ill|lt=100 Heritage Cultural Assets of Fisheries and Fishing Villages to Preserve for the Future|Q11519509|qid=Q11519509|1=100 Fishing Village Heritage Sites (Japan)}}{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.gyokou.or.jp/100sen/pdf/100sijitu/si060.pdf |format=PDF |title=100 Heritage Cultural Assets of Fisheries/Fishing Villages to Preserve for the Future: Ōse Shrine|publisher=National Fisheries Port and Fishing Ground Association |accessdate=2017-08-17}}. === Shrine name === It is often called by the common name '''Ōse Shrine''' (おせじんじゃ), and this name appears on the torii plaque. It is also called '''Ōse Myōjin'''. === Enshrined deity === * '''Hikite Chikara no Mikoto''' (ひきたぢからのみこと) Among major shrines considered shikinaisha, no others are known to enshrine a deity by this exact name. There is a theory identifying it with {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|qid=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} of the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|qid=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}, but while Ame-no-Tajikarao is generally treated as a mountain deity, Hikite Chikara no Mikoto is a protector of the sea, so the identification is uncertain. === Origin and history === [[画像:1500year Byakushin.JPG|thumb|right|Sacred tree of the shrine
One tree from the natural monument "Ōsezaki Byakushin Grove," estimated age 1,500 years.]] The founding period is unknown. One theory says that when a great earthquake in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|qid=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 13 ({{ill|lt=684|Q31128|qid=Q31128|1=684}}) suddenly uplifted the seabed by about 300 jō, creating an island called "Biwa-jima," people thought a deity had "pulled" land from {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|qid=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}, where much land had sunk in the same period's quakes, and began worshipping Hikite Chikara no Mikoto here. "Biwa-jima" later became tied to the mainland by sandspit formation and became {{ill|lt=Ōsezaki|Q11437688|qid=Q11437688|1=Cape Ose}}. In the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Tametomo|Q1339356|qid=Q1339356|1=Minamoto no Tametomo}}, {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|qid=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, and {{ill|lt=Hōjō Masako|Q463961|qid=Q463961|1=Hōjō Masako}} are said to have dedicated bows, arrows, helmet, mirror, and sword while praying for revival of the Minamoto line; after revival and establishment of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura shogunate|Q736839|qid=Q736839|1=Kamakura shogunate}}, many warriors dedicated bows and swords. In the Nanboku-chō period, {{ill|lt=Suzuki Shige-tomo|Q17219427|qid=Q17219427|1=Suzuki Shige-tomo}}, ancestor of the Enashi Suzuki clan, ruled this area (remains thought to be his residence survive) and is said to have devoted himself to shrine rites. Repeated earthquakes and tsunamis later destroyed both residence and offerings, but as some items were found in sand, local people enshrined them and the shrine was revived over time to the present. The present shrine buildings were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1939|Q18639|qid=Q18639|1=1939}} (Shōwa 14). The precinct includes {{ill|lt=Kami-ike of Ōse Myōjin|Q11437694|qid=Q11437694|lt=Kami-ike|1=Kamiike}}, one of the {{ill|lt=Seven Wonders of Izu|Q11380538|qid=Q11380538|1=Seven Mysteries of Izu}}. It draws devotion as a place of purification and as a guardian deity of the sea. It is also widely known as a scenic point looking across Suruga Bay to {{ill|lt=Mt. Fuji|Q39231|qid=Q39231|1=Mount Fuji}} and {{ill|lt=Mt. Ashitaka|Q3247941|qid=Q3247941|1=Mount Ashitaka}}, and as a major {{ill|lt=scuba diving|Q1096878|qid=Q1096878|lt=diving|1=Scuba diving}} destination. There is a custom of dedicating red {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=fundoshi|1=UNKNOWN}} for maritime safety, and a former custom of dedicating scale models of newly launched fishing boats for safe voyages and good catches. Many ema paintings of fishing scenes and wooden boat models were dedicated, and some are exhibited in the ema hall. A fire in 1892 (Meiji 25) destroyed models dedicated before then. As of 2008, some of 32 later-dedicated boats are displayed; ink inscriptions on their undersides record donor name/address/year, accurately reflecting fishing-vessel styles of Suruga Bay coasts in Meiji, Taishō, and early Shōwa, and showing the shrine's faith sphere spread widely around Suruga Bay. === Ōse Festival === [[ファイル:Cape Ose and Mount Fuji.jpg|サムネイル|300px|{{ill|lt=Ōsezaki|Q11437688|qid=Q11437688|1=Cape Ose}}, site of Hikite Chikara no Mikoto Shrine]] '''Ōse Festival''' (おせまつり) is the annual grand festival of Hikite Chikara no Mikoto Shrine, held every April 4. On the day, neighboring communities prepare fishing boats decorated with large catch-flags and cedar/cherry twigs, and young men board in flamboyant women's dress and head to the shrine by sea. Upon reaching the cove, "fortune bales" are thrown from the boats into the sea{{Refnest|group="注釈"|Filled with pebbles and dead leaves; said to symbolize stone weights for fishing nets{{Sfn|Miyata|p=29}}.}}, then first-year crew youths dive in to retrieve them and swim ashore, carrying them straight into the shrine precinct. Stones are then picked up from the beach and attached to boats, and they return while performing a dance called "Isami-odori" on board. The shrine itself calls it a "world-famous strange festival"[http://www.osezaki.jp/osematuri.html Ōse Coastal Commercial Association / Ōse Shrine Annual Grand Festival (Ōse Festival)], and it is considered a remnant of a community {{ill|lt=rite of passage|Q1131696|qid=Q1131696|1=Rite of passage}} among fishers{{Sfn|Miyata|pp=28-29}}. It is also said to be a type of {{ill|lt=Kashima dance|Q11677163|qid=Q11677163|1=kashima odo}}. On {{ill|lt=April 5|Q2507|qid=Q2507|1=April 5}}, {{ill|lt=1949|Q5188|qid=Q5188|1=1949}} (Shōwa 24), records note that {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōwa|Q34479|qid=Q34479|1=Hirohito}} and {{ill|lt=Empress Kōjun|Q230697|qid=Q230697|1=Empress Nagako}}, staying at the Numazu Imperial Villa, viewed the ceremonial big-catch dance performed on boats gathered offshore in front of the villa{{Cite book |和書 |author=Imperial Household Agency |title=Shōwa Tennō Jitsuroku Vol.10 |publisher=Tokyo Shoseki |year=2017-03-30 |page=799|isbn=978-4-487-74411-4 }}. Today, the "Uchiura Fishing Port Festival" is also held at the same time, including seafood/local products sales and taiko performances. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"|30em}} === Sources === {{Reflist|18em}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|author= Miyata Noboru|authorlink=宮田登|title = Between History and Folklore from perspectives of sea and city|date = 1996-11-10|publisher = {{ill|lt=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755|qid=Q4346755|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}}|location = {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|qid=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Bunkyō|Q212713|qid=Q212713|1=Bunkyō}}|series = Historical Culture Library|volume = 2|language = Japanese|isbn = 4-642-05402-2|ref={{SfnRef|Miyata}} }} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Ōsezaki|Q11437688|qid=Q11437688|1=Cape Ose}} *{{ill|lt=Kami-ike of Ōse Myōjin|Q11437694|qid=Q11437694|1=Kamiike}} === External links === {{commonscat|Hikite Chikara no Mikoto Shrine}} *[http://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/kankou/asobu/osezaki/index.htm Numazu Tourism WEB : Ōsezaki] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hikite Chikara no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hikite Chikara no Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]
